title: Tool-output redirection — token gate, handle, and bounded part
description: The token-based externalization gate (replacing the byte cap), the first-class tool-output handle, and the redirected-part shape the serializer emits every send.

RedirectionGate.rule: DD-1/DD-2: externalize when estimatedTokens > min(ABS_FLOOR_TOKENS, HEADROOM_FRACTION * remainingWindowTokens). Replaces Truncate.MAX_BYTES (256*1024 bytes).
RedirectionGate.note: Defaults are the committed starting point; tune against live measurement.

ToolOutputHandle.note: DD-4: first-class handle, not a raw fs path string; resolvable by Read/Grep/recall and stable across compaction.

RedirectedToolPart.rule: DD-3: the prompt serializer emits {preview + handle reference} for this part on EVERY send; the full body is never re-inlined.
RedirectedToolPart.note: Replaces today's behaviour where an un-truncated result's full body is re-sent every turn.

TruncateResult.note: Byte-path → token-path; outputPath string → first-class handle.

notes: Built-in (grep/bash/read/registry/resolve-tools) and MCP results converge on the one seam (DD-4/DD-5).
Handle retention must outlive a compaction that collapses the producing turn.
